item.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

summary.text:
This resolution sets forth 13 articles of impeachment against President Donald Trump for high crimes and misdemeanors on charges of  

  1. war power-murder-piracy;
  2. militarization of domestic law enforcement;
  3. serial unconstitutional detentions and deportations;
  4. retaliation against constitutionally protected speech or association;
  5. abuse of the pardon power—sabotaging the rule of law;
  6. illegally crippling or defunding programs to protect consumers, the needy, workers, and the environment;
  7. usurpation of the congressional power of the purse;
  8. contempt of Congress—secret government;
  9. perverting law enforcement to persecute political opponents and benefit friends;
  10. suspending or dispensing with laws;
  11. flouting Section 1 of the Fourteenth Amendment;
  12. specious national emergency—foreign terrorist organization declarations; and
  13. domestic and foreign emoluments clauses.

bill.title: Impeaching Donald J. Trump, President of the United States, for high crimes and misdemeanors.

item.title: Impeaching Donald J. Trump, President of the United States, for high crimes and misdemeanors.
Impeaching Donald J. Trump, President of the United States, for high crimes and misdemeanors.

latestAction.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
